title: modes/fragments.rs: accessor fns duplicate enum-match code across four variants — boilerplate registry
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: DRY. Location: src/modes/fragments.rs:57-93 (four accessor fns). Violation: agency_fragment, quality_fragment, scope_fragment, modifier_fragment are four near-identical match-on-enum-to-const functions. They exist purely to dispatch enum variant to const &str. This is the kind of boilerplate a proc macro or a derive would eliminate. Mandated Refactor: Define a trait PromptFragment { const FRAGMENT: &'static str; } or method fn fragment(&self) -> &'static str on each enum, implemented via a small macro_rules! that takes (variant, path) pairs. Or use the strum crate's EnumDiscriminants with an associated-const attribute. Result: adding a modifier is one line change, not four.
